content.problemStatement:
You are given a tree (connected, undirected, acyclic graph) consisting of NNN nodes. Based on this tree, you have to answer QQQ queries.

Each query is of the form:


K D V1 V2 ⋯ VKK\ D\  V_1\ V_2\ \cdots\ V_KK D V1​ V2​ ⋯ VK​ - output the number of pairs (i,j)(i, j)(i,j), 1≤i<j≤K1 \leq i \lt j \leq K1≤i<j≤K, such that the shortest path between nodes ViV_iVi​ and VjV_jVj​ in the tree has DDD edges.
constraints: The sum of KKK across all queries in a single test case is at most 10510^5105.
`ExplanationIn the first query, the pairs of nodes (2,3)(2, 3)(2,3) and (2,5)(2, 5)(2,5) have distance 222.
In the second query, there is no pair with distance 444.
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains two integers, NNN and QQQ.
N−1N - 1N−1 lines follow. Each line consists of two integers uuu and vvv, indicating that there is an edge between nodes uuu and vvv in the tree.
QQQ lines follow. Each line describes a query in the format given above.
content.outputFormat: For each query, output the answer on a new line.
